raw_consumer_statement: On XX/XX/year> I was purchasing a ticket to my sisters funeral from a company who said that they were XXXX XXXX. They changed me {$640.00} for which I immediately gave my credit card number. I explained to the agent ( XXXX XXXX ) that my son may be needing a ticket as well. She gave me what she said was her directly line for him to call. When I called my son to let him know about this, he said no thats too much I just got a ticket for less than half the price. He asked if I wanted him to get one for me at the much lower price. I agreed. Immediately I called back to XXXX XXXX telling to cancel the reservation. She was very angry and said she had to charge me a cancellation fee, of $ XXXX. I agreed. All of this took less that XXXX minutes. I called the credit card company- Citibank and said not to put through the charge for $ XXXX.The agent said that it had not hit the bank yet. I explained to him that I was leaving for the funeral and wanted to make sure that charge DID NOT go through. He told me dont worry XXXX XXXX , Citibank has your back. I was gone out of the XXXX  for 4 days. Upon my return I checked and they claimed nothing came through. The scammers reported the amount in two increments {$420.00} and another for $ XXXX I had started contacting the scammers several times a week as well as Citibank . The scammers even said that they would send me alive check for the full amount. Spoke to several people at both places Then the scammers disconnected the 2 numbers which they had given to me. And Citibank kept sending me letters and telling me to contact the merchant. All the emails to Citibank are not going through after checking several times as to what they are. Please help me get my money back. They are also using incorrect dates.
